name: issue-411-natvent-floor-exit-consistency
description: Reproduces the Issue #411 timeline shape: a monitored window/door sensor stays open all night while WHF nat-vent runs, and indoor drifts down toward comfort_heat as the outside air cools. Before the fix, apply_classification()'s Phase 2 proactive floor exit and nat_vent_temperature_check()'s reactive hard-floor exit competed as two independent decision-makers for the same threshold, and Phase 2 additionally performed a sensor-blind HVAC-mode restore (arming the day classification's mode, e.g. 'cool', even though the window was still open) on top of _deactivate_fan()'s own restore -- producing a confusing exit/re-entry narrative every time the automation cycle re-evaluated. After the fix, all nat-vent exit paths route through the single _exit_nat_vent() choke point, which checks the monitored sensor before deciding restore-vs-pause -- with the sensor open all night, the session exits exactly once and stays paused (no HVAC mode restored into an open window, no thrash).
notes: Occupant impact: the resident left a bedroom window open overnight per the nat-vent recommendation. The physical temperature stayed within expected tolerance the whole night -- WHF nat-vent was cycling exactly as designed. The problem was NOT a real comfort failure; it was that the system's own log narrative and internal HVAC-mode bookkeeping told a story inconsistent with what was actually happening (repeated 'exit nat-vent, arm cool mode, immediately re-enter nat-vent' cycles) and a benign, expected cycling dip risked being misreported as a comfort incident.

Config: fan_mode=whole_house_fan (matches the #411 report's WHF setup); comfort_heat=65F is the overnight floor; thermal_model.k_passive=-0.5 with confidence=high lets Phase 2's ODE-projected time-to-floor cross the MIN_VIABLE_NAT_VENT_HOURS=1.0h threshold partway through the night, matching the shape of the #411 log's proactive-exit-then-confusion timeline.

IMPORTANT residual-gap finding from constructing this scenario (reported to the Coordinator, not silently patched): the paused-by-door reactivation gate at check_natural_vent_conditions() re-evaluates the shared 4-part condition (_nat_vent_may_reactivate()) purely from INSTANTANEOUS outdoor/indoor/comfort_heat/threshold values -- it has no knowledge that Phase 2 just predicted (30 min earlier) that indoor will cross comfort_heat within the hour. If indoor is still numerically above comfort_heat at the very next automation cycle after a proactive exit, the reactivation gate can re-arm nat-vent for exactly one cycle before Phase 2 immediately re-predicts the same floor breach and exits again -- a real, still-present one-cycle thrash that Issue #411's Pass-4 fix does NOT close (the fix unifies the shared *ceiling* sub-condition across sites and the exit-side handoff; it does not make the entry-side reactivation gate floor-prediction-aware). This scenario's temperature curve is deliberately tuned so indoor is AT/BELOW comfort_heat by the very next tick after the proactive exit (00:00: 64.5F < 65F floor) -- closing that one-cycle window for THIS scenario's specific curve shape by satisfying the reactivation gate's own floor guard (indoor > comfort_heat) becoming false. This is a scenario-construction choice documented here, not evidence that the underlying one-cycle race is fixed in general; a shallower approach to the floor (smaller |k_passive|, or a temp_update spaced closer to the proactive-exit tick) can still reproduce one exit/reactivate/exit cycle. Recommend filing this as an explicit follow-up issue if the coordinator agrees it should be closed rather than accepted as scope_not_covered on #411.

VERIFIED SCENARIO EFFECTIVENESS (three-exercise protocol, CLAUDE.md): (a) if _exit_nat_vent() were deleted and Phase 2 reverted to its old hand-rolled deactivate+restore+grace sequence, the 23:30 assertion would still show the session ending, but the engine would incorrectly attempt _set_hvac_mode(c.hvac_mode) ('cool', from the day classification) and _set_temperature_for_mode() while the sensor is still open -- the nat_vent_not_active assertion would still pass by label, but a companion check on the action log (an unconditional 'cool' mode write appearing while the sensor remains open) would fail, and _paused_by_door would incorrectly stay False since the old code never checked the sensor before restoring. This scenario's real teeth are verifying engine_state (_paused_by_door=True, _natural_vent_active=False) stays consistent through the whole night, not just the event label at 23:30.

(b) Two-way alignment (single engine, Issue #236): this scenario drives tools/sim_harness/ against the REAL production AutomationEngine -- it exercises _exit_nat_vent()'s sensor-open branch (Issue #411 Pass 4 plan) exactly as documented in docs/08-COMPUTATION-REFERENCE.md's unified nat-vent exit handoff section (Scribe-owned, updated in the same PR).

(c) User outcome if this scenario fails to catch a regression: the occupant would again see the activity log narrate a confusing sequence of nat-vent exits and HVAC mode changes that contradict each other, eroding trust in the daily briefing and investigator reports exactly as CLAUDE.md's 'Daily Briefing as Primary UI' principle warns against -- even though the physical comfort outcome was fine both nights.

No HVAC mode write to 'cool' (the day classification's mode) may appear in the action log at any point while the sensor remains open -- this is the specific sensor-blind-restore bug fixed by routing Phase 2 through _exit_nat_vent().

Momentary in-tolerance dips below comfort_heat during active nat-vent (e.g. the 03:49/06:00 readings here) must not be reported as comfort incidents -- that gate lives at the COORDINATOR level (_is_nat_vent_tolerated_deviation, coordinator.py), which this AutomationEngine-only harness does not drive; it is tested directly in tests/test_incident_detection.py's TestComfortViolationIncident / TestComfortViolationsMinutesNatVentGate suites instead.
Promote to golden/ once reviewed and confirmed by the user per the Golden Simulation Test Policy.

events.note: Evening classification: warm day, day-mode hvac_mode='cool' -- this is the value Phase 2 used to sensor-blindly restore before the fix. Comfort band armed [65/78].
Evening: indoor 74F, outdoor 68F -- outdoor cooler than indoor, above comfort_heat floor.
Window opens per recommendation. outdoor 68F < indoor 74F, indoor > comfort_heat 65F, outdoor <= threshold 81F -- nat-vent activates, WHF turns on, HVAC suppressed to off.

Late evening: indoor has cooled to 70F, outdoor 63F. passive_rate = -0.5*(70-63) = -3.5F/hr; time_to_floor = (70-65)/3.5 = 1.43h -- still >= MIN_VIABLE_NAT_VENT_HOURS(1.0h), so Phase 2 does not exit yet. Session continues uninterrupted, window still open.

Indoor has drifted down to 67F, 2F above the comfort_heat floor (65F). passive_rate = -0.5*(67-60) = -3.5F/hr; time_to_floor = (67-65)/3.5 = 0.57h < MIN_VIABLE_NAT_VENT_HOURS(1.0h) -- Phase 2's proactive floor exit fires. The monitored window sensor is STILL OPEN at this point (nobody closed it), so _exit_nat_vent()'s sensor-open branch must pause rather than sensor-blindly restore 'cool' mode.

30 min after the proactive exit: indoor has continued its natural passive decay down to 64.5F -- now AT/BELOW the comfort_heat floor (65F) even with the fan off. Deliberately tuned so the paused-by-door reactivation gate's own floor sub-condition (indoor > comfort_heat) fails on this very next re-evaluation (see the residual-gap note above for why this matters). Window sensor is still open -- the engine must remain paused (_paused_by_door=True), not re-arm nat-vent or restore active HVAC.

Matches the #411 report's ~03:49 timestamp shape. Indoor has settled to 64F (below comfort_heat 65F) -- the exact benign, expected cycling dip that risked a false comfort_undertemp incident in production (coordinator-side fix, tested separately in tests/test_incident_detection.py). At the AutomationEngine level, the session remains paused and consistent -- no thrash.

Early morning, window still open, indoor continues to sit below the floor. Engine must still be in the single, consistent paused state established at 23:30 -- no additional nat_vent_predicted_floor_exit events, no oscillation, no reactivation.

assertions.reason: Sensor opens with outdoor 68F < indoor 74F and outdoor <= threshold 81F -- nat-vent activates, WHF turns on, HVAC suppressed to off.

Phase 2 proactive floor exit fires (time_to_floor 0.57h < 1.0h threshold, indoor 67F) -- _exit_nat_vent() is called and _natural_vent_active becomes False. With the monitored sensor still open, the sensor-open branch pauses (_paused_by_door=True) rather than sensor-blindly restoring the day classification's 'cool' mode.

verdict.summary: With a monitored window sensor open all night, Phase 2's proactive floor exit correctly pauses the session (via the unified _exit_nat_vent() choke point) instead of sensor-blindly restoring an active HVAC mode into an open window, and -- for this scenario's specific temperature curve -- the session stays in one consistent paused state overnight with no exit/re-entry thrash and no false comfort incident.
verdict.observed_behavior: classification_applied -> natural_ventilation on window open (20:10) -> session runs uninterrupted through the evening as indoor cools -> nat_vent_predicted_floor_exit + pause at 23:30 (indoor 67F, 0.57h from the 65F floor, window still open) -> engine remains consistently paused through 06:00 with indoor settling to 63.5F -- no HVAC mode restored while the sensor is open, no repeated exit/re-entry cycling for this curve shape.
verdict.expected_behavior: _exit_nat_vent() is the single choke point for every nat-vent exit path (Phase 2 proactive, reactive hard-floor, outdoor-reversal, outdoor-too-warm). With the monitored sensor open, it always pauses (_paused_by_door=True, _pre_pause_mode captured, restore_hvac=False) rather than restoring the day classification's HVAC mode -- eliminating the sensor-blind double-restore that produced the #411 log's contradictory 'exit nat-vent, arm cool, immediately re-enter nat-vent' narrative. NOTE: a residual, still-present one-cycle reactivation race at the entry-side gate (not addressed by #411's scope) is documented in the notes above -- this scenario's temperatures are tuned to avoid triggering it, not proof that it cannot occur under other curve shapes. Momentary, expected cycling dips below comfort_heat during active nat-vent are not reported as comfort incidents (verified separately at the coordinator level in tests/test_incident_detection.py).
